hierarchy.title: भारतीय ररज़र्व बैंक RESERVE BANK OF INDIA
hierarchy.text:
भारतीय ररज़र्व बैंक RESERVE BANK OF INDIA
RBI/DPSS/2024-25/123 CO.DPSS.OVRST.No.S447/06-26-002/2024-25 July 30, 2024
The Chairman / Managing Director / Chief Executive Officer Authorised Non-bank Payment System Operators


Madam / Dear Sir,
Master Directions on Cyber Resilience and Digital Payment Security Controls for non-
bank Payment System Operators
The safety and security of payment systems is a key objective of the Reserve Bank of India
(RBI). To ensure that the authorised non-bank Payment System Operators (PSOs) are
resilient to existing and emerging information systems and cyber security risks, it was
announced in the Statement on Developmental and Regulatory Policies issued as part of
Monetary Policy Statement dated April 08, 2022 that RBI will issue directions on Cyber
Resilience and Payment Security Controls for Payment System Operators (PSOs).
2.
Accordingly, a draft Master Direction was published on June 02, 2023 seeking
comments / feedback from stakeholders. Based on the feedback received, it has been decided
to issue the final Directions, covering robust governance mechanisms for identification,
assessment, monitoring and management of these risks. The Directions also cover baseline
security measures for ensuring system resiliency as well as safe and secure digital payment
transactions. However, they shall endeavour to migrate to latest security standards. The
existing instructions on security and risk mitigation measures for payments done using cards,
Prepaid Payment Instruments (PPIs) and mobile banking continue to be applicable as hitherto.
In case of any discrepancy in applicability of guidelines, the instructions provided in this Master
Direction shall prevail.
भुगतान और ननपटान प्रणाली निभाग, केंद्रीय कायाालय, 14 िी मंनिल, केंद्रीय कायाालय भिन,शहीद भगत न ंह मागा, फोटा, मुम्बई - 400001
फोनTel: (91-22) 2260 1000; फैक् Fax: (91-22) 2265 9566; ईमेल-e-mail : cgmdpssco@rbi.org.in
Department of Payment and Settlement Systems, Central Office, 14th Floor, Central Office Building, Shahid Bhagat Singh Road, Fort, Mumbai -400001
नहंदी आ ान है, इ का प्रयोग बढाइए
3.
 These Directions are issued under Section 10 (2) read with Section 18 of Payment
and Settlement Systems Act, 2007 (Act 51 of 2007).
Yours faithfully,
(Sudhanshu Prasad)
Chief General Manager
Index Page Section I - Preliminary 4 Introduction 4 Short Title and Commencement 4 Applicability 5 Purpose 5 Section II - Governance Controls 5 Cyber Security Preparedness 5 Risk Assessment and Monitoring 6 Section III - Baseline Information Security Measures / Controls 6 Inventory Management 6 Identity and Access Management 6 Network Security 7 Application Security Life Cycle 8 Security Testing 8 Vendor Risk Management 9 Data Security 9 Patch and Change Management Life Cycle 10 Incident Response 10 Business Continuity Plan (BCP) 10 Application Programming Interfaces (APIs) 11 Employee Awareness / Training 11 Cloud Security 11 Other Security Measures 12 Section IV - Digital Payment Security Measures / Controls 13 Mobile Payments 14 Card Payments 14 Prepaid Payment Instruments 15 Acronyms 16
Master Directions on Cyber Resilience and Digital Payment Security Controls for non-
bank Payment System Operators (PSOs)
Section I
Preliminary
Introduction
1.
In exercise of the powers conferred under Section 10 (2) read with Section 18 of the
Payment and Settlement Systems Act, 2007 (PSS Act), the Reserve Bank of India (RBI, Bank)
being satisfied that it is necessary and expedient in the public interest so to do, issues the
Master Directions hereinafter specified.
Short Title and Commencement
2.
These Master Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Cyber Resilience
and Digital Payment Security Controls for non-bank PSOs) Master Directions, 2024 (Master
Directions, Directions).
3.
The Directions shall come into effect on the day they are placed on the official website
of the RBI. In order to provide adequate time to put in place the necessary compliance
structure, a phased implementation approach1 is prescribed as under -
Regulated Entity Timeline Large non-bank PSOs2 April 1, 2025 Medium non-bank PSOs3 April 1, 2026 Small non-bank PSOs4 April 1, 2028
1 The timelines prescribed in the instructions issued earlier by RBI shall continue to be applicable as hitherto.

2 For the purpose of these Directions, Clearing Corporation of India Limited (CCIL), National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI), NPCI Bharat Bill Pay Limited, Card Payment Networks, Non-bank ATM Networks, White Label ATM Operators (WLAOs), Large PPI Issuers, Trade Receivables Discounting System (TReDS) Operators, Bharat Bill Payment Operating Units (BBPOUs) and Payment Aggregators (PAs) are considered as large non-bank PSOs.

3 Cross-border (in-bound) Money Transfer Operators under Money Transfer Service Scheme (MTSS) and Medium PPI Issuers are considered as medium non-bank PSOs.

4 Small PPI Issuers and Instant Money Transfer Operators are considered as small non-bank PSOs.

Categorisation of authorised non-bank PPI Issuers into small, medium and large is as per the Oversight Framework for Financial Market Infrastructures (FMIs) and Retail Payment Systems (RPSs). If a PPI Issuer moves to a higher category, the timeline of the category to which it moves into, would apply. For instance, if a small (or medium) PPI issuer moves into medium category (or large), it will need to comply with these Directions within a period of two (or one) years from the time of new categorisation, as the case may be.
Applicability
4.
The provisions of these Directions shall apply to all authorised non-bank PSOs.
5.
To effectively identify, monitor, control and manage cyber and technology related risks
arising out of linkages of PSOs with unregulated entities who are part of their digital payments
ecosystem (like payment gateways, third party service providers, vendors, etc.), PSOs shall
ensure adherence to these Directions by such unregulated entities as well, subject to mutual
agreement. An organisational policy in this respect, approved by the Board, shall be put in
place.
Purpose
6.
These Directions aim to improve safety and security of the payment systems operated
by PSOs by providing a framework for overall information security preparedness with an
emphasis on cyber resilience.
Section II
Governance Controls
7.
The Board of Directors (Board) of the PSO shall be responsible for ensuring adequate
oversight over information security risks, including cyber risk and cyber resilience.
However, primary oversight may be delegated to a sub-committee of the Board, headed
by a member with experience in information / cyber security, which shall meet at least
once every quarter.
8.
The PSO shall formulate a Board approved Information Security (IS) policy to manage
potential information security risks covering all applications and products concerning
payment systems as well as management of risks that have materialised. The policy shall
be reviewed annually. It shall cover at the minimum, (i) roles and responsibilities of Board
/ sub-committees of the Board, senior management and other key personnel; (ii)
measures to identify, assess, manage and monitor cyber security risk which shall also
include various types of security controls for ensuring cyber resiliency along with
processes for training and awareness of employees / stakeholders.
Cyber Security Preparedness
9.
The PSO shall prepare a distinct Board approved Cyber Crisis Management Plan (CCMP)
to detect, contain, respond and recover from cyber threats and cyber attacks. Relevant
guidelines from CERT-In / National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre
(NCIIPC) / IDRBT and other agencies may be referred for guidance.
Risk Assessment and Monitoring

hierarchy.title: 10. The Board shall entrust the responsibility and accountability for implementing the IS policy
hierarchy.text:
10. The Board shall entrust the responsibility and accountability for implementing the IS policy
and the cyber resilience framework as well as for continuously assessing the overall IS
posture of PSO to a senior level executive with expertise in areas of information security
including cyber security. [e.g. Chief Information Security Officer (CISO)].

hierarchy.title: 11. The PSO shall define appropriate Key Risk Indicators (KRIs) to identify potential risk
hierarchy.text:
11. The PSO shall define appropriate Key Risk Indicators (KRIs) to identify potential risk
events and Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) to assess the effectiveness of security
controls. These KRIs and KPIs shall be continuously monitored by the sub-committee of
the Board referred to in paragraph 7.

hierarchy.title: 12. IT assessment reports (such as system audit, VA / PT reports, etc.) shall be placed before
hierarchy.text:
12. IT assessment reports (such as system audit, VA / PT reports, etc.) shall be placed before
the sub-committee responsible for IT oversight in the meeting due immediately after such
report is available.

hierarchy.title: 13. The PSO shall, undertake a cyber risk assessment exercise relating to launch of new
hierarchy.text:
13. The PSO shall, undertake a cyber risk assessment exercise relating to launch of new
product / services / technologies or undertaking major changes to infrastructure or
processes of existing product / services. Action points emanating from such assessment
shall be implemented under the oversight of the CISO or equivalent executive.
Section III
Baseline Information Security Measures / Controls

hierarchy.title: 14. Inventory Management
hierarchy.text: 14. Inventory Management

children.title: (a) The PSO shall maintain a record of all the key roles, information assets (applications,
children.text:
(a) The PSO shall maintain a record of all the key roles, information assets (applications,
data, infrastructure, personnel, services, etc.), critical functions, processes and third-
party service providers, and classify and document their levels of usage, criticality
and business value.

children.title: (b) A complete process flow diagram of network resources, inter-connections and
children.text:
(b) A complete process flow diagram of network resources, inter-connections and
dependencies, and data flows with other information assets, including any other third-
party systems, shall be created and maintained.

children.title: (c) Asset information shall necessarily include an identifier, network address, asset
children.text:
(c) Asset information shall necessarily include an identifier, network address, asset
location, asset owner name and End of Life Support (EoLS). All assets (hardware or
software) approaching EoLS shall be assessed to evaluate risks associated with the
continued use of the unsupported asset.

hierarchy.title: 15. Identity and Access Management
hierarchy.text: 15. Identity and Access Management

children.title: (a) Policies, procedures and controls that address access privileges as well as
children.text:
(a) Policies, procedures and controls that address access privileges as well as
administration of access rights must be established.

children.title: (b) All individuals having access to the IT environment of the PSO shall be assigned a
children.text:
(b) All individuals having access to the IT environment of the PSO shall be assigned a
digital identity, which shall be maintained and monitored till termination.

children.title: (c) Default authentication settings in systems / software / services shall be deactivated
children.text:
(c) Default authentication settings in systems / software / services shall be deactivated
and changed before they are rolled out to live environment.

children.title: (d) Access to systems and different environments (development, test, production, etc.)
children.text:
(d) Access to systems and different environments (development, test, production, etc.)
shall be based on need-to-have, need-to-know and based on the principle of least
privilege5.

children.title: (e) The use of privileged accounts6 shall be with multi-factor authentication and tightly
children.text:
(e) The use of privileged accounts6 shall be with multi-factor authentication and tightly
monitored. Appropriate controls, including rotation policy, shall be implemented.

children.title: (f) Necessary security controls, including centralised mechanism to whitelist / blacklist,
children.text:
(f) Necessary security controls, including centralised mechanism to whitelist / blacklist,
shall be put in place to ensure secure use of removable media and portable devices
(eg. smartphones, laptops, etc.).

children.title: (g) In case of remote / work from home situations, adequate precautions, including multi-
children.text:
(g) In case of remote / work from home situations, adequate precautions, including multi-
factor authentication mechanism, shall be in place.

children.title: (h) The PSO shall define and implement procedures that limit, lock and terminate system
children.text:
(h) The PSO shall define and implement procedures that limit, lock and terminate system
and remote sessions after a pre-defined period of inactivity.

children.title: (i) PSO shall have physical and environmental safeguards, with periodic testing, to
children.text:
(i) PSO shall have physical and environmental safeguards, with periodic testing, to
protect access to its information assets from natural disasters and other threats.

hierarchy.title: 16. Network Security

children.title: (a) Network devices shall be configured and checked periodically for security rules;
children.text: (a) Network devices shall be configured and checked periodically for security rules;

children.title: (b) A Security Operations Centre (SOC) shall ensure proactive and centralised
(c) Automated mechanisms (eg. Security Information and Event Management (SIEM)
(d) Anti-malware solutions shall be implemented so as to prevent, detect and contain
children.text:
(d) Anti-malware solutions shall be implemented so as to prevent, detect and contain
malware attacks by scanning all incoming data to prevent malware from being
installed and infecting a system;
5 Principle of least privilege refers to granting a user or a process only those privileges or access to the specific data, resources and applications that are/is necessary for intended function or to complete a required task.
6 Privileged accounts refer to a user account that has more privileges than ordinary user e.g. Administrator level accounts. Such user may be able to install or remove software, upgrade the operating system, or modify system or application configurations. They might also have access to files that are not normally accessible to standard users.

children.title: (e) Multi-layered boundary defences shall be incorporated into IS systems to efficiently
(f) Network segmentation shall be made based on role, location and environment
children.text:
(f) Network segmentation shall be made based on role, location and environment
(production, testing, development, etc.) to segregate systems and data of varying
criticality; Whitelisting solutions shall be in place to ensure that only permitted
applications and services with validated needs are running. Whitelisting of ports may
also be ensured with constant monitoring; and

children.title: (g) PSOs shall allow devices (such as laptop, desktop, mobile, etc.) to be connected to
children.text:
(g) PSOs shall allow devices (such as laptop, desktop, mobile, etc.) to be connected to
its network only after ensuring that they meet the prescribed security measures /
requirements.

hierarchy.title: 17. Application Security Life Cycle (ASLC)
hierarchy.text: 17. Application Security Life Cycle (ASLC)

children.title: (a) The PSO shall follow a 'secure by design' approach such as Secure-Software
children.text:
(a) The PSO shall follow a 'secure by design' approach such as Secure-Software
Development Life Cycle (S-SDLC) for design and development of products / services
and ensure that no security weaknesses are introduced during the build process.

children.title: (b) The PSO shall implement a multi-tier application architecture, that ensures
children.text:
(b) The PSO shall implement a multi-tier application architecture, that ensures
segregation of database layer from other layers, while developing digital payment
products and services.

children.title: (c) The ASLC guidelines shall apply to procured products / services as well. Further, the
children.text:
(c) The ASLC guidelines shall apply to procured products / services as well. Further, the
PSO shall obtain the source code of all critical applications procured from third-party
vendors. In case obtaining source code is not possible, there shall be an escrow
arrangement for the source code to ensure continuity of services.

hierarchy.title: 18. Security Testing
hierarchy.text: 18. Security Testing

children.title: (a) The PSO shall ensure that all its applications are subjected to rigorous security
children.text:
(a) The PSO shall ensure that all its applications are subjected to rigorous security
testing, such as source code review, VA, PT, etc., through qualified professionals at
adequate frequency (at least on annual basis) in authenticated mode.

children.title: (b) If the source code is not owned by the PSO, it shall obtain a certificate from the
children.text:
(b) If the source code is not owned by the PSO, it shall obtain a certificate from the
application developer stating that the application is free of vulnerabilities, malwares
and any covert channels in the code. Fresh certificate shall be obtained for any
changes to source code.

children.title: (c) Deficiencies reported in the security testing shall be resolved in a time bound manner.
children.text:
(c) Deficiencies reported in the security testing shall be resolved in a time bound manner.
Any recurring observation shall necessarily be reported to the Board sub-committee
responsible for IT oversight along with detailed analysis for recurrence and resolution.

children.title: (d) Any deployment or redeployment of new or existing services supporting critical
children.text:
(d) Any deployment or redeployment of new or existing services supporting critical
functions, applications and infrastructure components must be done only after
conducting security audits, including VA / PT, and addressing the resultant
observations.

hierarchy.title: 19. Vendor Risk Management
hierarchy.text:
19. Vendor Risk Management
The PSO shall be guided by the Framework for Outsourcing of Payment and Settlement-
related Activities by PSOs issued by RBI vide circular dated August 03, 2021 (updated
from time to time).

children.title: (a) The PSO shall put in place necessary security controls for preventing infiltration into
children.text:
(a) The PSO shall put in place necessary security controls for preventing infiltration into
its network from vendor environments.

children.title: (b) The PSO shall adhere to the relevant legal and regulatory requirements relating to
children.text:
(b) The PSO shall adhere to the relevant legal and regulatory requirements relating to
geographical location of infrastructure and processing, storage and usage of data
and also ensure compliance by their vendors.

children.title: (c) In case of vendors involved in critical processes and activities7, the PSO shall obtain
children.text:
(c) In case of vendors involved in critical processes and activities7, the PSO shall obtain
certified assurance from an independent auditor on the vendor's cyber resilience
capabilities.

hierarchy.title: 20. Data Security
hierarchy.text: 20. Data Security

children.title: (a) The PSO shall put in place a comprehensive data leak prevention policy for
children.text:
(a) The PSO shall put in place a comprehensive data leak prevention policy for
confidentiality, integrity, availability and protection of business and customer
information (both in transit and at rest) in respect of data available with it or at vendor
managed facilities, commensurate with the criticality and sensitivity of the information
held / transmitted.

children.title: (b) The PSO shall employ suitable mechanism to ensure traceability and visibility of data
children.text:
(b) The PSO shall employ suitable mechanism to ensure traceability and visibility of data
assets.

children.title: (c) The PSO shall develop and implement an Information Security Management System
children.text:
(c) The PSO shall develop and implement an Information Security Management System
(ISMS) based on applicable standards.

children.title: (d) Application and database security controls shall focus on secure handling,
children.text:
(d) Application and database security controls shall focus on secure handling,
processing, storage and protection of data, in particular, Personally Identifiable
Information (PII). Data in transit and rest shall be secured through either data or
channel encryption or both.

children.title: (e) The PSO storing card (debit / credit / prepaid) data shall adhere to PCI-DSS
children.text:
(e) The PSO storing card (debit / credit / prepaid) data shall adhere to PCI-DSS
guidelines and obtain PCI-DSS certification.

children.title: (f) The PSO shall have necessary systems and procedures in place to periodically (at
children.text:
(f) The PSO shall have necessary systems and procedures in place to periodically (at
least on a half-yearly basis) test the backed-up data to ensure recovery without loss
of transactions or audit-trails.
7 Critical processes as defined in the Framework for Outsourcing of Payment and Settlement-related Activities by PSOs issued by RBI vide circular dated August 03, 2021 (updated from time to time).

hierarchy.title: 21. Patch and Change Management Life Cycle
hierarchy.text: 21. Patch and Change Management Life Cycle

children.title: (a) The PSO shall put in place a documented policy and process to identify and
children.text:
(a) The PSO shall put in place a documented policy and process to identify and
implement patches to technology and software assets released by OEMs / others.

children.title: (b) Security patches shall be applied to the relevant systems and applications within an
children.text:
(b) Security patches shall be applied to the relevant systems and applications within an
appropriate time frame from their release. In case of critical patches released to tackle
well-known / reported attacks, the PSO shall have a mechanism to apply them
immediately.

children.title: (c) Any change to system, technology, application, source code, etc., shall be managed
children.text:
(c) Any change to system, technology, application, source code, etc., shall be managed
using robust change management processes and after ensuring that overall integrity
of the IT set-up is not compromised.

children.title: (d) Patches and changes shall be implemented in production environment after testing
children.text:
(d) Patches and changes shall be implemented in production environment after testing
and validating the same in other environments (e.g. development, testing, etc.).

hierarchy.title: 22. Incident Response
hierarchy.text: 22. Incident Response

children.title: (a) The PSO shall put in place a Board approved incident response mechanism, which
children.text:
(a) The PSO shall put in place a Board approved incident response mechanism, which
shall include provisions to promptly notify its senior management, relevant employees
and regulatory, supervisory and relevant public authorities, of cyber incidents.

children.title: (b) Response strategies shall incorporate readiness to meet various incident scenarios
children.text:
(b) Response strategies shall incorporate readiness to meet various incident scenarios
based on situational awareness and potential impact, consistent communication and
co-ordination with stakeholders.

children.title: (c) Post-incident analysis, including forensic analysis (wherever necessary), shall be
children.text:
(c) Post-incident analysis, including forensic analysis (wherever necessary), shall be
conducted to determine the impact and root cause of incidents. Adequate measures
shall be taken to avoid recurrence of similar incidents.

children.title: (d) Unusual incidents like cyber-attacks, outage of critical system / infrastructure, internal
children.text:
(d) Unusual incidents like cyber-attacks, outage of critical system / infrastructure, internal
fraud, settlement delay, etc., shall be reported to RBI in the Incident Reporting Format
(Annex 1) within 6 hours of detection. Indicative list of types of incidents to be reported
is in Annex 2. Any cyber security incident shall also be reported to CERT-In.

hierarchy.title: 23. Business Continuity Plan (BCP)
hierarchy.text: 23. Business Continuity Plan (BCP)

children.title: (a) The PSO shall develop a BCP based on different cyber threat scenarios, including
children.text:
(a) The PSO shall develop a BCP based on different cyber threat scenarios, including
extreme but plausible events to which it may be exposed. It shall be reviewed at least
once a year and include a comprehensive cyber incident response, resumption and
recovery plan, to manage cyber security events or incidents.

children.title: (b) The BCP shall be designed to enable rapid recovery from any adverse event and
children.text:
(b) The BCP shall be designed to enable rapid recovery from any adverse event and
facilitate safe resumption of critical operations aligned with Recovery Time Objective
(RTO) and Recovery Point Objective (RPO) while ensuring the security of processes
and data.

children.title: (c) The PSO shall strive to achieve near-zero RPO.
children.text: (c) The PSO shall strive to achieve near-zero RPO.

children.title: (d) The PSO shall set up a Disaster Recovery (DR) facility in a different seismic zone
children.text:
(d) The PSO shall set up a Disaster Recovery (DR) facility in a different seismic zone
than the Primary Data Centre (PDC). There shall be a defined methodology for
reconciliation of data so as to ensure that there is no data loss while resuming
operations from the DR.

children.title: (e) DR drills shall be conducted on a half-yearly or more frequent basis. Any divergence
children.text:
(e) DR drills shall be conducted on a half-yearly or more frequent basis. Any divergence
from the RTO and RPO shall be analysed and the deficiency be rectified on urgent
basis.

hierarchy.title: 24. Application Programming Interfaces (APIs)
hierarchy.text: 24. Application Programming Interfaces (APIs)

children.title: (a) To safeguard applications against risks emanating from insecure APIs, the PSO shall
children.text:
(a) To safeguard applications against risks emanating from insecure APIs, the PSO shall
put in place, inter-alia, the following measures:

children.title: (i) Authentication and Authorisation - Establish identity of the communicating
children.text:
(i) Authentication and Authorisation - Establish identity of the communicating
applications;

children.title: (ii) Confidentiality - Ensure that the message content is not tampered with;
children.text: (ii) Confidentiality - Ensure that the message content is not tampered with;

children.title: (iii) Integrity - Resources are reliably transferred; and
children.text: (iii) Integrity - Resources are reliably transferred; and

children.title: (iv) Availability and Threat Protection - APIs are available when needed; anomalous8
children.text:
(iv) Availability and Threat Protection - APIs are available when needed; anomalous8
activities identified and mitigative action taken.

children.title: (b) The PSO shall adhere to relevant standards and globally recognised frameworks on
children.text:
(b) The PSO shall adhere to relevant standards and globally recognised frameworks on
API security.

hierarchy.title: 25. Employee Awareness / Training
hierarchy.text: 25. Employee Awareness / Training

children.title: (a) The PSO shall ensure periodic repeated training / awareness programs on information
children.text:
(a) The PSO shall ensure periodic repeated training / awareness programs on information
security issues for its employees and vendors managing its information assets.

children.title: (b) A system of periodic evaluation of cyber security awareness amongst employees
children.text:
(b) A system of periodic evaluation of cyber security awareness amongst employees
shall be operationalised. Employees with an awareness level below a benchmark
score may be restricted / prohibited from accessing information assets.

children.title: (c) Board members and key senior management personnel shall be provided training
children.text:
(c) Board members and key senior management personnel shall be provided training
and sensitised on information security and cyber risks.

hierarchy.title: 26. Cloud Security
hierarchy.text: 26. Cloud Security

children.title: (a) The PSO subscribing to cloud services shall put in place a cloud operation policy (as
children.text:
(a) The PSO subscribing to cloud services shall put in place a cloud operation policy (as
a part of board approved Information Security Policy), highlighting, inter-alia, activities
that can be located in cloud servers, clearly identified roles and responsibilities for
cloud service provider (CSP), data localisation, protection and recoverability
requirements.
8 Activity that is sufficiently different than historical activity.

children.title: (b) It shall be ensured that data pertaining to the PSO is clearly segregated and stringent
children.text:
(b) It shall be ensured that data pertaining to the PSO is clearly segregated and stringent
access control measures are implemented. Multi-tenancy environments shall be
protected against data integrity and confidentiality risks, and against co-mingling of
data.

children.title: (c) The PSO shall ensure that the CSP is subjected to periodic (at least annually)
children.text:
(c) The PSO shall ensure that the CSP is subjected to periodic (at least annually)
independent information and cyber security audits. Such audit reports and the CSPs
overall performance, its cyber resilience capabilities shall be reviewed annually by the
Board sub-committee responsible for IT oversight.

hierarchy.title: 27. Other Security Measures
hierarchy.text: 27. Other Security Measures

children.title: (a) The PSO shall ensure that all payment transactions, including cash withdrawals,
children.text:
(a) The PSO shall ensure that all payment transactions, including cash withdrawals,
involving debit to the account conducted through electronic modes (bank accounts /
debit cards / credit cards / PPIs, etc.) are permitted only by validation through multi-
factor authentication, except where explicitly permitted / relaxed.

children.title: (b) The PSO shall ensure secure configuration of its entire IT infrastructure. It shall also
children.text:
(b) The PSO shall ensure secure configuration of its entire IT infrastructure. It shall also
equip its servers with adequate security measures so that unauthorised / spoofed
transactions are not done and the authentication process is robust, secure and
centralised.

children.title: (c) The PSO shall put in place a real-time / near real-time fraud monitoring solution to
children.text:
(c) The PSO shall put in place a real-time / near real-time fraud monitoring solution to
identify suspicious transactional behaviour and generate alerts.

children.title: (d) The PSO shall have a manned facility to function on 24x7x365 basis for facilitating
children.text:
(d) The PSO shall have a manned facility to function on 24x7x365 basis for facilitating
swift resolution of unauthorised / fraudulent transactions reported by customers and
also to provide prompt response to Law Enforcement Agencies (LEAs).

children.title: (e) The PSO shall put in place a mechanism to capture, analyse, store and archive audit
children.text:
(e) The PSO shall put in place a mechanism to capture, analyse, store and archive audit
logs in a systematic manner. Log messages shall provide relevant information to
uniquely identify the user that initiated an action, the action and parameters of that
particular action. Access to log data shall be provided on a controlled basis. Audit
logs shall be preserved for a period of at least five years.

children.title: (f) The PSO shall ensure that the payment architecture operated by them is robust,
children.text:
(f) The PSO shall ensure that the payment architecture operated by them is robust,
scalable and commensurate with the transaction volumes; this shall be reviewed by
the sub-committee of the Board.

children.title: (g) The PSO shall employ secure mail and messaging systems to ensure that inbound
children.text:
(g) The PSO shall employ secure mail and messaging systems to ensure that inbound
and outbound traffic through mail, messages or any other media are secure.

children.title: (h) The PSO shall subscribe to anti-phishing / anti-rogue app services for identifying and
children.text:
(h) The PSO shall subscribe to anti-phishing / anti-rogue app services for identifying and
taking down phishing websites / rogue applications.

children.title: (i) The PSO shall, on an ongoing basis, either directly or through its participants and
children.text:
(i) The PSO shall, on an ongoing basis, either directly or through its participants and
service providers, create public awareness on precautionary measures to safeguard
against frauds and cyber threats while using digital payment products.
Section IV
Digital Payment Security Measures / Controls
In addition to the extant instructions applicable to PSOs for digital payment transactions, the
following instructions shall also be applicable.

hierarchy.title: 28. The PSO shall facilitate its members / participants have mechanisms for online alerts
hierarchy.text:
28. The PSO shall facilitate its members / participants have mechanisms for online alerts
based on various parameters such as failed transactions, transaction velocity, as well as
in case of new account parameters (eg., excessive activity), time zone, geo-location, IP
address origin (in respect of unusual patterns, prohibited zones / rogue IPs), behavioural
biometrics, transaction origination from point of compromise, transactions to mobile
wallets / mobile numbers / VPAs on whom vishing or other types of fraud are registered /
recorded, declined transactions, transactions with no approval code, etc.

hierarchy.title: 29. While sending SMS / e-mail alert or any other notification to customers, either by PSO or
hierarchy.text:
29. While sending SMS / e-mail alert or any other notification to customers, either by PSO or
payment system participants, the following shall be ensured -

children.title: (a) Bank account number / card number / other confidential information are redacted /
children.text:
(a) Bank account number / card number / other confidential information are redacted /
masked to the extent possible.

children.title: (b) Online payment transactions shall mention merchant name (not the payment gateway
children.text:
(b) Online payment transactions shall mention merchant name (not the payment gateway
/ aggregator) and amount; for fund transfers, name of the beneficiary and debit
amount. The PSO shall ensure that the name is taken from the system of the entity
maintaining the beneficiary account.

children.title: (c) In cases where the OTP is a factor of authentication, the PSO shall ensure that the
children.text:
(c) In cases where the OTP is a factor of authentication, the PSO shall ensure that the
OTP is mentioned at the end of the notification message and the message shall also
refer the specific transaction.

hierarchy.title: 30. The PSO shall provide a facility on its mobile application / website that would enable
hierarchy.text:
30. The PSO shall provide a facility on its mobile application / website that would enable
customers, with necessary authentication, to identify / mark a fraudulent transaction for
seamless and immediate notification to the issuer of payment instrument. It shall also
ensure facilitation of such mechanism by the system participants.
Mobile Payments

hierarchy.title: 31. The PSO providing / facilitating / processing mobile payment services transactions shall
hierarchy.text:
31. The PSO providing / facilitating / processing mobile payment services transactions shall
comply with the following security practices and risk mitigation measures and shall also
ensure that the participants in its payment system comply with these instructions:

children.title: (a) PSO shall put in place a mechanism to ensure that the mobile application is free from
children.text:
(a) PSO shall put in place a mechanism to ensure that the mobile application is free from
any anomalies or exceptions for which the application was not programmed.

children.title: (b) The PSO shall ensure that an authenticated session, together with its encryption
children.text:
(b) The PSO shall ensure that an authenticated session, together with its encryption
protocol, remains intact throughout an interaction with the customer. In case of any
interference or if the customer closes the application, the session shall be terminated,
and the affected transactions resolved or reversed out.

children.title: (c) The PSO shall ensure device binding9 / finger printing of mobile applications with the
children.text:
(c) The PSO shall ensure device binding9 / finger printing of mobile applications with the
device and SIM. In case the mobile application remains unused beyond a policy
determined specified period, the PSO shall ensure device binding is performed again.

children.title: (d) The PSO shall ensure that an online session on mobile application is automatically
children.text:
(d) The PSO shall ensure that an online session on mobile application is automatically
terminated after a fixed period of inactivity and customers are prompted to re-login.

children.title: (e) The PSO shall, where applicable, set down the maximum number of failed log-in or
children.text:
(e) The PSO shall, where applicable, set down the maximum number of failed log-in or
authentication attempts after which access to the mobile application is blocked. There
shall be a secure procedure to re-activate the access to blocked product / service.
The customer shall be notified for failed log-in or authentication attempts,
immediately.

children.title: (f) The PSO shall put in place a control mechanism, to identify any presence of remote
children.text:
(f) The PSO shall put in place a control mechanism, to identify any presence of remote
access applications (to the extent possible) and prohibit access to the mobile
payment application while the remote access is live.

children.title: (g) Whenever there is a change in registered mobile number or email ID linked to the
children.text:
(g) Whenever there is a change in registered mobile number or email ID linked to the
payment instrument there shall be a cooling period of minimum 12 hours before
allowing any payment transaction through online modes / channels.
Card Payments

hierarchy.title: 32. The PSO shall ensure that terminals installed at merchants for capturing card details for
hierarchy.text:
32. The PSO shall ensure that terminals installed at merchants for capturing card details for
payments or otherwise are validated against the PCI-P2PE program; PoS terminals with
PIN entry installed at the merchants for capturing card payments (including the double
swipe terminals) shall be approved by the PCI-PTS program.

hierarchy.title: 33. The card networks shall facilitate implementation of transaction limits at card, Bank
hierarchy.text:
33. The card networks shall facilitate implementation of transaction limits at card, Bank
Identification Number (BIN) as well as at card issuer level. Such limits shall mandatorily
9 The device binding shall be preferably implemented through a combination of hardware, software and service information.
be set at the card network switch itself. The card networks shall institute an alert
mechanism on a 24x7x365 basis, to be triggered to the card issuer in case of any
suspicious incident. Card networks shall ensure that card details of the customers are
stored in an encrypted form at any of their server locations. They shall also ensure that
processing of the card details in readable format is performed in a secure manner.
Prepaid Payment Instruments

hierarchy.title: 34. PPI issuers are encouraged to communicate OTP and transaction alerts with users in a
hierarchy.text:
34. PPI issuers are encouraged to communicate OTP and transaction alerts with users in a
language of their choice, including vernacular languages.

hierarchy.title: 35. The PPI issuers - banks and non-banks - shall put in place suitable cooling period for
hierarchy.text:
35. The PPI issuers - banks and non-banks - shall put in place suitable cooling period for
funds transfer and cash withdrawal after such funds are electronically loaded on to the
PPI.
Acronyms
API Application Programming Interface ASLC Application Security Life Cycle ATM Automated Teller Machine BCP Business Continuity Plan BIN Bank Identification Number CCMP Cyber Crisis Management Plan CERT-In Indian Computer Emergency Response Team CISO Chief Information Security Officer CSP Cloud Service Provider DR Disaster Recovery EoLS End of Life support IDRBT Institute for Development and Research in Banking Technology IP Internet Protocol IS Information System ISMS Information Security Management System IT Information Technology KPI Key Performance Indicator KRI Key Risk Indicator LEA Law Enforcement Agency NCIIPC National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre OEM Original Equipment Manufacturer OTP One Time Password PCI Payment Card Industry PCI-DSS Payment Card Industry-Data Security Standard PCI-P2PE Payment Card Industry-Point to Point Encryption PCI-PTS Payment Card Industry-PIN Transaction Security PDC Primary Data Centre PIN Personal Identification Number PoS Point of Sale PPI Prepaid Payment Instrument PSO Payment System Operator PSS Payment and Settlement Systems PT Penetration Testing RBI Reserve Bank of India RPO Recovery Point Objective RTO Recovery Time Objective SDLC Software Development Life Cycle S-SDLC Secure-Software Development Life Cycle SIEM Security Information and Event Management SIM Subscriber Identification Module SMS Short Message Service SOC Security Operations Centre VA Vulnerability Assessment VPA Virtual Payment Address WLAO White Label ATM Operator
